name: Swedish Flatbread (Bakestone Recipes)
recipeInstructions:
1. You may need as much as 12 fl total. Blend the flours together with the salt.
2. Mix in sufficient water to bind together into a dough – the quantity will depend on the types of flour used.
3. Beat until the dough leaves the sides of the bowl, then turn onto a floured board and knead thoroughly.
4. Heat the bakestone over a moderate heat and grease it.
5. Divide the dough into four and roll one quarter out into a round as thin as possible.
6. Using a plate about 8 inches wide, trim the edges into a neat circle.
7. Prick all over to prevent the dough bubbling while cooking.
8. Transfer to the bakestone and cook over a moderate heat for about 15 minutes, or until slightly colored. Turn and cook the other side.
9. Repeat with the rest of the dough, working up the trimmings for re-shaping and baking.
10. Cool on a wire tray. When cold, store in an airtight container (Danish-butter-cookie containers, the big ones, are good for this).
